title: Tryst with the Constitution: The Supreme Court needs structural reform, not just more judges
description: Earlier this week, the Union Cabinet approved a proposal to increase the sanctioned strength of the Supreme Court from 34 to 38 judges. The stated objective underlying the proposal is to ensure that the Court “functions more efficiently and effectively ensuring speedy justice.”The justification is familiar. It is often argued that increasing judicial strength would improve disposal rates, reduce backlog and enable the Court to deal more effectively with its ever-expanding docket. The logic appea
